message:
test: pin semaphore.h conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_semaphore.rs covering 7 functions:
- sem_init / sem_destroy: 4 initial values (0/1/5/100)
- sem_post: 3 posts from 0 → value 3
- sem_wait: 3 waits from 5 → value 2
- sem_trywait at zero: fails on both
- sem_trywait at 1: succeeds on both
- sem_timedwait: past-expiry absolute timeout fails
- sem_getvalue: cross-impl value match

Each impl manages its own opaque sem_t (32 bytes). Named semaphores
deferred (shm cleanup complexity).

7 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.

Bead: bd-sem1

Cardinality budget on per-symbol metrics.","notes":"2026-02-13 progress (SunnyOx): implemented structured symbol-classification logging in support-matrix maintenance gate path. check_support_matrix_maintenance now emits JSONL events at target/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance.log.jsonl with fields {timestamp,trace_id,level,event,mode,api_family,symbol,outcome,errno,artifact_refs,details}; includes coverage_summary, status_counts, status_validation_issue (WARN for GlibcCallThrough issues), policy outcomes, gate_result, and opt-in TRACE per-symbol status snapshots via FRANKENLIBC_SYMBOL_GATE_TRACE=1. Validation: scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ; scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh --strict ; FRANKENLIBC_SYMBOL_GATE_TRACE=1 scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh ; bash -n scripts/check_support_matrix_maintenance.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42.487346784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:12:16.029065549Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:12:16.029023050Z","close_reason":"Structured symbol logging contract is implemented and now regression-tested (comment #575).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","logging","observability","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldj.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1122,"issue_id":"bd-ldj.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed bd-ldj.8 logging closure pass by adding deterministic regression coverage for support-matrix structured symbol logging.

Code changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/support_matrix_maintenance_test.rs` with:
  - JSONL loader + gate runner helpers
  - `maintenance_gate_emits_structured_logs_with_required_fields`
  - `maintenance_gate_trace_flag_emits_symbol_status_snapshot_events`
  - lock guard to serialize gate-writer tests and avoid log/report races
- Tests assert required logging fields (`timestamp`, `trace_id`, `level`, `event`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `outcome`, `errno`, `artifact_refs`, `details`), `gate_result`, WARN classification semantics for `GlibcCallThrough`, and TRACE opt-in behavior.

Verification:
1) rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test support_matrix_maintenance_test -- --nocapture
   - PASS (6 passed, 0 failed).
2) cargo fmt --check --package frankenlibc-harness
   - PASS after formatting.
3) rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-harness --all-targets
   - FAIL due unrelated in-flight `frankenlibc-core/src/string/regex.rs` compile errors (`Ast::Repeat` missing `greedy`; `CompiledRegex` missing `last_error`/`pattern`).
4) rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-harness --all-targets -- -D warnings
   - FAIL due same unrelated regex refactor breakage and existing workspace lint debt outside this bead.

Representative failure signatures:
- error[E0559]: variant `Ast::Repeat` has no field named `greedy`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `last_error`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `pattern`

Coordination:
- Registered `MagentaFalcon` in Agent Mail, confirmed empty ack-required inbox, and posted cross-agent status update in thread `coord-2026-02-25-magentafalcon`.

Documented; not failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-LOCALE-001 documented; bd-locl2 opened for fl::newlocale mask validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","locale"]}
-{"id":"bd-locl2","title":"FIX: fl::newlocale should reject category_mask with LC_ALL bit (DISC-LOCALE-001)","description":"POSIX 2008 newlocale: category_mask must be a bitwise OR of LC_*_MASK constants. The LC_ALL bit (1<<6 = 64) is not a valid mask bit; glibc returns NULL+EINVAL when it is set. fl::newlocale currently accepts it and returns a non-null locale_t. Fix: validate category_mask & ~LC_ALL_MASK_VALID == 0 in locale_abi.rs newlocale.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","locale"]}

Fix: validate category_mask & ~LC_ALL_MASK_VALID == 0 in locale_abi.rs newlocale.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:01:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:01:00Z","close_reason":"Reject invalid newlocale category-mask bits with EINVAL","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","locale"]}

 {"id":"bd-luc3d","title":"printf_conformance executor ignores length modifiers (%Lf/%td/%zd/%jd); causes varargs width mismatch","description":"crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs run_host_snprintf takes PrintfArg::Int and passes c_int to libc::snprintf, but when the format specifier uses length modifiers %td (ptrdiff_t), %zd (size_t), %jd (intmax_t), %ld, %lld, glibc reads the arg at the wider width and gets garbage upper bits. Fix: when parsing args, inspect the format string for length modifiers and promote numeric args to PrintfArg::Long before dispatch. Surfaced by sprintf_t_d in bd-12hh matrix (expected '-12345', host returned '4294954951' due to stack-read overflow).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:32:32.945225484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.859074014Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.856763492Z","close_reason":"promote_args_by_format() scans fmt specifiers and promotes Int->Long/Ulong where length modifier demands 64-bit. 8 integer length-modifier cases removed from KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS. %Lf/%Le remain skipped (f80 has no Rust equivalent). Committed d7ee870.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-mjon","title":"bd-yos subtask: Thread bootstrap trampoline finalization for pthread_create","description":"Background:
- Thread bootstrap is a core blocker for both callthrough removal and TLS key APIs.

Goal:
- Finalize clone/trampoline startup path for pthread_create with explicit invariants and ABI boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Thread start trampoline contract (entry args, return handling, cleanup path).
2) Parent/child synchronization at startup.
3) Error path behavior and errno propagation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pthread_create success/failure semantics match declared support matrix behavior.
- No hidden host-libc dependency in startup path for covered mode/profile.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for bootstrap components and error branches.
- Structured logs: trace_id, thread_id, parent_id, start_state, exit_state, errno, timing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonForge","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.822186049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139407950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139340033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":1123,"issue_id":"bd-mjon","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Thread bootstrap trampoline implementation.

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs: Added clone_thread_asm (inline asm trampoline for Linux clone syscall)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added SYS_GETTID, SYS_SET_TID_ADDRESS, sys_gettid, sys_exit_thread, sys_clone_thread
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: Complete native thread creation via clone (ThreadHandle, create_thread, join_thread, detach_thread, self_tid)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mod.rs: Updated re-exports

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core: PASS
- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::thread: 6/6 PASS

Tests:
- create_and_join_thread_returns_value
- child_thread_can_write_shared_memory
- multiple_threads_created_and_joined
- detach_thread_does_not_error
- join_null_handle_returns_einval
- gettid_returns_positive

Architecture:
- Uses CLONE_VM|CLONE_FS|CLONE_FILES|CLONE_SIGHAND|CLONE_THREAD|CLONE_SYSVSEM|CLONE_SETTLS|CLONE_PARENT_SETTID|CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID
- Child stack: [fn_ptr][arg] layout, trampoline pops both, calls fn_ptr(arg), SYS_exit on return
- Join uses futex-wait on TID (kernel clears via CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID and wakes waiters)
- Parent/child sync via startup futex (ensures args consumed before create returns)
- 2MiB stack with 4KiB guard page via mmap","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mtj","title":"EPIC: E2E Reality Gate (build + LD_PRELOAD smoke + ABI surface)","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Goal: make glibc_rust indisputably real by proving it builds and runs on real binaries.

Deliverables:
- CI/local gate: cargo fmt/check/clippy/test + cdylib build.
- ABI surface reports: exported symbol list + versioning + support matrix.
- LD_PRELOAD smoke harness for strict+hardened.

Acceptance:
- One command runs the full gate and returns non-zero on any drift/regression.
- Failures produce a deterministic crash bundle (backtrace + evidence snapshot).

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:36:06.684806153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346791885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:51:07.346754184Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critique","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-144","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2cr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-2p0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3ex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-3jh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mtj","depends_on_id":"bd-6yd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ni101","title":"iconv ABI: stack overflow on invalid UTF-8 under mode=hardened","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test iconv_abi_test hardened_mode_iconv_invalid_utf8_reports_eilseq` aborts with:

```
thread 'hardened_mode_iconv_invalid_utf8_reports_eilseq' (...) has overflowed its stack
fatal runtime error: stack overflow, aborting
...(signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduces reliably on Linux x86_64 with the current tree (commit a47dd54e, 2026-04-22). Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly (noted in UPGRADE_LOG.md 2026-04-22).

## Test

`crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/iconv_abi_test.rs:822` `hardened_mode_iconv_invalid_utf8_reports_eilseq`

The test wraps a standard iconv call in `with_mode("hardened", ...)` which sets `FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened` via env var, then feeds invalid UTF-8 (`[0xC3, 0x28]`) through UTF-8 -> UTF-16LE and expects `ICONV_ERROR` + `errno == EILSEQ`. The strict-mode counterpart (line ~805) passes fine.

## Likely site

The core iconv path (`crates/frankenlibc-core/src/iconv/mod.rs`) has no mode-specific branching — it just decodes and emits EILSEQ cleanly. So the recursion must originate in the ABI layer's `runtime_policy::decide` or one of its downstream kernel hooks for `ApiFamily::Locale` + `SafetyLevel::Hardened`. `decide` wraps the kernel call in `panic::catch_unwind` but stack overflow isn't a panic, so the unwind guard doesn't catch it.

## Fix direction

1. Run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` under `ulimit -s unlimited` to get a deep backtrace and confirm the recursion site.
2. Audit any re-entrant calls in the Locale-family hardened path of the membrane kernel (runtime_math::* observers, scaled_cost, observe -> decide loop, etc.).
3. Likely candidates: an observer re-triggers decide, or a hardened-mode callback re-enters via `set_abi_errno` -> locale lookup -> iconv ABI -> decide.

## Out of scope

Not a dep-update regression (pins are at latest stable; same failure was noted by the cod session on 2026-04-22 before any of cc-libc's commits).

## Provenance

First documented in UPGRADE_LOG.md 2026-04-22 exhaustive-refresh session. Not previously tracked as a bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:48:49.728849045Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:08:53.495104775Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:08:53.491975655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","hardened","iconv","membrane","stack-overflow","testing"]}

 {"id":"bd-nklt","title":"D.2: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement gai_suspend() which waits for one or more async DNS requests to complete, with optional timeout.

## POSIX Signature
```c
int gai_suspend(const struct gaicb *const list[], int nitems, const struct timespec *timeout);
```

Returns:
- 0: at least one request completed
- EAI_AGAIN: timeout expired before any completion
- EAI_ALLDONE: all requests already completed
- EAI_SYSTEM: error (errno set)

## Design
1. Scan the request list — if any are already completed (gai_error != EAI_INPROGRESS), return 0 immediately
2. If all are completed, return EAI_ALLDONE
3. Otherwise, wait using a futex on a shared completion counter:
   - Each request increments a completion futex when it transitions out of IN_PROGRESS
   - gai_suspend does FUTEX_WAIT on the counter with timeout
   - On wake, re-scan the list for completed requests
4. If timeout is NULL, wait indefinitely (block until at least one completes)
5. If timeout expires, return EAI_AGAIN

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs
- Replace gai_suspend body
- Add ASYNC_DNS_COMPLETION_FUTEX: AtomicU32 (incremented on each completion)
- Use SYS_futex for timed wait

## Testing
- All requests already done: returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Empty list (nitems=0): returns EAI_ALLDONE
- Null list: returns EAI_SYSTEM with EINVAL
- Timeout with no completions: returns EAI_AGAIN (use short 1ms timeout)
- One request completes immediately: returns 0

## Acceptance Criteria
gai_suspend correctly waits and returns appropriate status codes. Timeout is respected. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:58.266246587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:22.166397683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.578636240Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No gai_suspend futex wait needed — function returns immediately since no async requests exist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-nnjiz","title":"[MEDIUM] malloc::elimination::symmetric_workload_exceeds_target_success_rate flakes at ~24% under shared rch-worker load","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-core --lib` fails one test:

```
thread 'malloc::elimination::tests::symmetric_workload_exceeds_target_success_rate' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-core/src/malloc/elimination.rs:967
expected symmetric workload success rate above 30%:
  EliminationStats { attempts: 6726, successes: 1600, success_rate_ppm: 237882, ... }
test result: FAILED. 1217 passed; 1 failed
```

Measured 23.79% vs 30% threshold.

## Diagnosis

The test runs a symmetric producer/consumer workload and checks the elimination-backed allocator publish/match success rate stays above 30%. On a loaded rch worker (shared compute, other agents' builds hitting the same CPUs), the timing windows that allow matches to succeed get squeezed, and the measured rate drops into the 20s%.

This is the same category as the noted flake in UPGRADE_LOG.md 2026-04-22 session (`free_matches_waiting_consumer_through_elimination`) — perf-sensitive concurrency tests with hardcoded thresholds misfire under heterogeneous host load.

The raw stats show this is not a correctness failure: 98-113 matches out of ~100 publishes per slot is a 98%+ slot-local match rate. The low 24% global rate comes from many **collisions** (5268-5329 per slot) where a waiting consumer got a hit-but-raced-out. That's the expected cost of high contention, not a regression in the elimination algorithm itself.

## Fix direction

Three legitimate options, pick one:

1. **Raise tolerance**: lower the assertion threshold from 30% to 20% and annotate why. Acceptable because the true regression signal is 'publishes/slot > 0 and matches/slot > 0'; the 30% figure was baked in on a less-loaded test host.
2. **Normalize against collisions**: assert `(successes + collisions) / attempts > 80%` (i.e. the elimination path is *exercised*), not that every attempt succeeds. That tracks what the test is actually trying to verify.
3. **Mark as `#[ignore]` under CI with a doc note**: run explicitly via `cargo test -- --ignored` on hardware-isolated runners only. Least preferred because it hides the flake.

Do **not** add `--test-threads=1` — the test is *about* concurrency.

## Blast radius

Single test, flaky only under shared-host load. Other 1217 tests in the same invocation pass. Adjacent tests in the elimination module pass.

## Verification

- Reproduced once on vmi1149989 at 23.79% (237882 ppm).
- Same test has historically passed on the same worker when idle (per UPGRADE_LOG.md notes).

 {"id":"bd-s83dv","title":"fuzz_pathname harness: leaks realpath(NULL) return instead of freeing","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs:153-158 calls realpath(cs.as_ptr(), NULL), gets a malloc'd buffer, and deliberately leaks it (comment: 'leak in the fuzz target is acceptable'). LeakSanitizer flags this and exits with 77 as soon as a path bytes combination makes realpath succeed (resolving to something short, e.g. 2 bytes). Effect: short fuzz runs fail on an infrastructural leak, masking any real library-side leak. Fix: unsafe { libc::free(ret as *mut c_void) } after archetype completes. Our realpath at stdlib_abi.rs:4050 uses libc::malloc so libc::free is the right pair. Reproducer: crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_pathname/leak-f413061db6eee949e03cba98bbb1b4526ec3525c","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:01:39.200746584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:06:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: free realpath/canonicalize_file_name returns with libc::free in fuzz_pathname harness.
